steps.title: Understand what no solution means for linear systems
steps.content: A system of linear equations has no solution when the two equations represent parallel lines — they have identical slopes but different y-intercepts. Geometrically, parallel lines never intersect.

steps.title: Rewrite the first equation to identify its form
steps.content:
Given $3x + 9y = 24$, divide both sides by 3:
$$x + 3y = 8$$
Any equation parallel to this will have the form $x + 3y = k$ where $k \neq 8$. If $k = 8$, the equations are identical (infinite solutions); if $k \neq 8$, they are parallel (no solution).

steps.title: Check each choice for the parallel form
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x + 3y = 8$: $x+3y=8$ is exactly the simplified original equation, so it gives infinitely many solutions, not no solution.
  • Choice B — $x + 3y = -14$: $x+3y=-14$ has the same left side but a different constant, so the lines are parallel and distinct.
  • $9x -3y = 0$ and $9x + 3y = 24$: their coefficient ratios do not match $1:3$, so each would intersect the original line once.
This matches, so B is correct.
